This tool creates a basic rent receipt format for general use. For HRA tax claims or legal disputes, verify the exact format required with a CA or tax professional. We do not provide legal or tax advice.

Receipt data is saved only in your browser (localStorage) if you click Save Draft.

Advertisement space

Ad space reserved for future

What is a Rent Receipt?

A rent receipt is a document issued by a landlord to a tenant confirming that rent has been paid. In India, rent receipts are commonly used to claim House Rent Allowance (HRA) exemption under Section 10(13A) of the Income Tax Act.

When Do You Need a Rent Receipt?

  • To claim HRA exemption while filing income tax returns (ITR)
  • To submit proof of rent to your employer for salary processing
  • As a legal record of rent payment between tenant and landlord
  • For bank loan applications that require proof of residence

HRA Exemption Rules (General Guide)

  • HRA is exempt under Section 10(13A) if you live in rented accommodation
  • If annual rent exceeds ₹1,00,000, the landlord's PAN is mandatory
  • You cannot claim HRA if you own the property you live in
  • Rent receipts should ideally be monthly and signed by the landlord
  • Always consult a CA or tax professional for personalised advice

Important Disclaimer

JaldiDocs provides this tool for convenience only. This is not legal or tax advice. For accurate HRA claims and tax compliance, consult a Chartered Accountant or a tax professional. The format of rent receipts required by employers or tax authorities may differ.
